texts.full_text: e of individual autonomy and equal opportunity. If an employee's personal choices outside work hours do not harm their job performance or compromise workplace safety, should they really be subject to such scrutiny? On one hand, it may seem like a company is simply trying to maintain productivity by eliminating potential disruptions; on the other hand, drawing too stark of a line between professional and private lives could lead some individuals to feel unjustly targeted. The debate over mandatory drug testing often hinges upon two main arguments: protecting an employee's rights versus ensuring reliable statistics for workplace safety and health concerns. While it is crucial not to infringe on personal freedoms, especially those protected under federal law (such as prescribed marijuana use), there must also be a balance struck between individual liberties and maintaining safe working environments where accidents caused by drug-induced impairment are minimized. To navigate this delicate equilibrium effectively will require thoughtful consideration of each party involved - employers seeking to protect their workforce while upholding ethical standards; employees asserting their rights without compromising the well-being of themselves or others around them, and lawmakers crafting legislation that respects both employer needs and employee protections equally. texts.full_text: abolic and vascular processes are so tightly interconnected, any disruption in one will necessarily impact the other. AlthouGh altered ATBF is one consequence of expanding fat tissue mass during obesity or with age-related changes (a condition often referred to as lipodystrophy), it's also a contributing factor that can exacerbate metabolic dysregulation. Research indicates several mechanisms by which adipose tissue blood flow may be regulated: 1. Endothelial Function and Vasomotion: Endothelium-derived factors such as nitric oxide (NO) play critical roles in vasodilation, thus affecting ATBF directly through the regulation of vessel diameter. 2. Metabolic Demand Signals: Adipocytes release various metabolites that can influence blood flow; for instance, free fatty acids and glycerol might alter vascular tone. 3. Hormonal Influences: Hormones like insulin or catecholamines have been shown to affect adipose tissue perfusion through their actions on the vasculature directly (e.g., via adrenergic receptors) as well as indirectly by altering metabolic activity within fat cells. 4. Inflammatory Mediators and Cytokines: In obesity, increased levels of inflammatory cytokines such as TNF-alpha can lead to impaired endothelial function which may reduce ATBF. 5. Neural Regulation: Sympathetic nervous system innervation affects adipose tissue blood flow through the release of norepinephrine that acts on alpha-adrenergic receptors in vascular smooth muscle, typically causing vasoconstriction but also having complex interactions depending upon receptor subtype and local context. 6. Structural Changes with Adiposity: As fat mass increases beyond a certain threshold (as seen during obesity), structural changes such as fibrosis or hypoxia within the adipose tissue can lead to impaired perfusion. Understanding these regulatory mechanisms is crucial, not only for grasping how ATBF contributes under normal physiological conditions but also in pathological states. This knowledge could pave ways toward therapeutic interventions

texts.full_text: f angel investors, and entrepreneurs. Amy Millman, CEO of Springboard Enterprises, and serial entrepreneur Shoba Purushothaman (2002 Springboard alum), were seeking advice about the state of angel and venture investing in New Delhi, and in general in India, with no preconceived outcome in mind. Springboard Enterprises is a venture catalyst organization that selects, trains and presents women entrepreneurs to raise venture capital. We were delighted to meet with Padmaja Ruparel, president of the Indian Angel Network (IAN) which is 300 members strong, and Geetika Dayal, executive director 100X Entrepreneurship, who had been working on a project with Springboard Enterprises. The first impression we had was that India is very much a country of young people. The median age in the United States is 36.7 years, while in India it’s only 25.3 years. (Source: CIA World Factbook) IAN has a membership base of entrepreneurs who are under 30 years old. The members are mostly self made entrepreneurs. And they’re also very well connected to each other, through common friends, common schools, and family networks. The second impression we had was that in India the angel investor is different from what we see here in the United States. Here in the US, an angel investor is someone who provides capital for a start-up company and actively participates in its development, usually by contributing intellectual or technical expertise. In India, the angel investor is someone who is a wealthy individual who can provide capital and mentorship to an entrepreneur, and also can help that entrepreneur connect to other investors. There are few formal structures for angel investing in India, so it’s very much based on trust. And third, we had the impression that there is a lack of access to capital for entrepreneurs. In the US, there are many different types of capital available to entrepreneurs, from friends and family to bank loans to angel and venture capital. But in India, there are few options. There are n

texts.full_text: ce data throughput rates. In conventional HARQ communication systems, the receiver on receiving a burst stores the bursts in a memory. An FEC decoder in the receiver performs an error correction decoding and transmission error check on each burst. Examples of the transmission error check, may include, but are not limited to, Cyclic Redundancy Check (CRC). The burst may be self-decodable, which may not require error correction decoding. If each bit in the burst is error free, then ARQ facilitates sending of an Acknowledgement (ACK) to the transmitter. However, if one or more bits in the burst have error, then a Negative Acknowledgement (NACK) is sent to the transmitter. If a NACK is received for a burst, the transmitter sends a retransmission for the burst. The receiver may soft combine the retransmission with the burst to generate a combined burst. A Signal/Noise (S/N) ratio of each bit in the combined burst is higher than the S/N ratio of a corresponding bit in each of the burst and the retransmission for the burst. A transmission error check is performed on the combined burst and if one or more bits in the combined burst have error, then a NACK is sent to the transmitter for the retransmitted burst. This is repeated until a burst in which each bit is error free is generated or until the maximum number of retransmissions is reached. Moreover, several bursts are transmitted simultaneously from the transmitter. Additionally, several ACK/NACK are received by transmitter, which retransmits a burst for every corresponding NACK. As a result, a large amount of memory is required for storing bursts and their corresponding retransmissions. The older bursts cannot be stored for longer duration of time as memory space required for storing the retransmissions often leads to space constraints. Additionally, typical H-ARQ memory management schemes uses a fixed number of queues for data transfer and each such queue has a fixed size corresponding to maximum possible burst size. texts.full_text: e Chuck and Kim Mulligan The Winemaking Bug Chuck caught the “winemaking bug” on a wine tasting excursion while vacationing in Santa Ynez. To his surprise, Chuck found he was able to detect and label the aromas and flavors in the wine (he had always loved to cook and had even considered becoming a chef). So he turned to his wife, Kim, and said, “Maybe I should start a small winery after I retire?!” To which the winery attendant replied, “Why wait? Our winemaker left IBM to study Enology.” Though he didn’t know the meaning of “enology”, the seed to become a winemaker had been planted. Chuck and Kim talked about the prospect of starting their oenology careers over dinner. Kim replied with a question: “You were saying how much you enjoyed all the time we spent tasting the wines. Which of the five wineries we visited did you think made the best Chuck replied, “No one was best overall. Each had one wine, but none had five outstanding wines. That’s the winery I would like to start!”. With that answer Kim agreed that he should go to college and become a Chuck entered the Enology program at Fresno State in 1981 and made the first Harmony Zinfandel in the summer of 1983. After working in various wineries and doing consulting, Chuck went back to school to receive his Enology degree from Fresno State. Kim was raised in Pleasanton, California and was a teacher for 25 years. With her encouraging spirit and mind, she has been a solid rock throughout all the challenging changes in Chuck’s career. Sexual harassment is a form of sex discrimination. Unwelcome sexual advances, requests for sexual favors, and other verbal or physical conduct of a sexual nature constitutes sexual harassment when submission to or rejection of this conduct explicitly or implicitly affects an employment, unreasonably interferes with an work performance or creates an intimidating, hostile or offensive work environment. Lawyers who handle sexual harassment cases represent individuals -- usually employees or students -- who have been victimized by unwanted verbal or physical conduct of a sexual nature, or subjected to sexually offensive behavior that creates an unfavorable environment. texts.full_text: , which is looking at the extent to which institutions and organisations in England failed to protect children from sexual abuse or dealt inadequately with its consequences when it came to light has called for more support structures within health services. The inquiry aims not only to uncover systemic failures but also seeks input directly rElated to how healthcare professionals, particularly those mentioned above (nurses, midwives and health visitors), can better facilitate a healing environment. These individuals often serve as the first point of contact or primary care providers who encounter victims during routine check-ups or when patients seek help due to physical symptoms that may be linked back to past traumas. The inquiry highlights how crucial it is for nurses specifically (given their frontline role) and all healthcare workers generally, to have specialized training on recognizing signs related not just purely medical conditions but also potential indicators of abuse. Additionally, the process encourages them in becoming more adept at providing emotional support while safeguarding confidentiality under strict ethical guidelines. texts.full_text: study was to compare TST and IGRA (Quantiferon-TB Gold) in detecting LTBI in refractory inflammatory disease patients prior to the initiation of a first TNFα inhibitor. A well-recognized problem in screening for LTBI is absence of a gold standard and thereby sensitivity and specificity of TST and IGRA cannot be directly measured. Nevertheless, assessment of tuberculosis exposure, combined with results of TST and IGRA, may roughly estimate the a priori chance of LTBI. Nine out of 56 patients (16.1%) tested for LTBI with either TST or IGRA. A concordant positive result was present in three patients with a medical history of tuberculosis exposure. The remaining six patients with discordance had either a negative TST and positive IGRA (one patient) or a positive TST and negative IGRA (five patients). The discordance in the five patieNts with positive TST and negative IGRA can be attributed to BCG vaccination (three patients) or a medical history of tuberculosis exposure (two patients). As for the patients with negative IGRA and positive TST, it cannot be excluded that IGRA may be false negative as the infection occurred in the distant past. This may be explained by the fact that IGRA mostly measures effector T cell responses whereas TST measures both effector and memory T cell responses. After 24 h

This was a prospective, observational study inclTo demonstrate sonographic detectable abnormalities of the posterior fossa in fetuses with a crown-rump length of 45-84 mm in high-risk pregnancies. This was a prospective, observational study including 250 high-risk pregnancies. The study was conducted in a tertiary care center, where 220 of the 250 pregnancies had a crown-rump length of 45-84 mm, the gestational age of 12-14 weeks. The study was aimed at identifying the feasibility of identifying posterior fossa malformations at this gestational age. The posterior fossa was examined for the presence of malformations, such as posterior fossa encephalocele, cerebellar hypoplasia, and posterior fossa encephaloclecele. The ultrasonographers, all of whom were experienced, were blinded to the clinical history of the patients to minimize the bias. The ultrasonographic parameters, such as cerebellar and posterior fossa dimensions, were measured and the results were analyzed. texts.full_text: rge Keeble George Keeble (26 September 1849 – 26 May 1923) was an English cricketer who played first-class cricket in a single match for Kent County Cricket Club in 1876. He was born at Southfleet in Kent and died at Dartford, also in Kent. Keeble appeared as an umpire in matches in 1874, including the Gentlemen v Players game, one of the fixtures of the season. As a right-handed tail-end batsman and a right-arm fast bowler in the round-arm style, he played for Kent against Sussex at Hove where his batting helped secure an unlikely draw after being nine wickets down on three occasions during their second innings chase against him! This impressive feat is one of many that will be remembered fondly by fans everywhere when they hear about George Keeble's career. It seems like he had quite the impact both with bat and ball despite only appearing briefly at this level; we can't help but wonder what other stories could have been written if given more opportunities over time—but

texts.full_text: e treatment paradigm towards using molecularly targeted agents. One of the most prominent targets in cancer treatment is kinases, which can be commonly targeted by small molecule inhibitors or monoclonal antibodies. Despite the initial enthusiasm in exploring kinaseinhibitors for GBM therapy due to their success in other cancers, progress has been slow and limited therapeutic efficacy has been observed thus far. This can be attributed to several factors including intratumoral heterogeneity, drug resistance mechanisms, and the complex signaling pathways involving multiple kinases within glioblastoma cells. To overcome these challenges and improve treatment outcomes for GBM patients, a shift in focus towards developing therapeutic strategies that target multiple kinases simultaneously is emerging as an attractive approach. By inhibiting several key kinase-driven pathways involved in cell proliferation, survival, and angiogenesis, it may be possible to disrupt the tumor microenvironment more effectively than with single agent therapies. Several multi-kinase inhibitors have shown promise in preclinical studies for glioblastoma treatment. For instance: 1) Dasatinib: A dual Src/ABL tyrosine kinase inhibitor, has demonstrated antiproliferative and proapoptotic effects against GBM cell lines by targeting multiple signaling pathways including the epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR)/PI3K/Akt pathway. 2) Imatinib: Though initially developed for chronic myelogenous leukemia, imatinib has also shown activity in inhibiting PDGF-B autocrine loops that promote glioblastoma cell proliferation and invasion. 3) Crizotinib and Eribulin: These agents inhibit MET kinase signaling which is often overexpressed or amplified in GBM, leading to increased tumor growth and invasiveness. 4) Sorafenib: A Raf/ROCK inhibitor that has been repurposed for glioblastoma therapy due to its ability to target VEGFR-2 involved in angiogenesis as well as other intracellular signaling pathways contributing to GBM progression. 5) Ponatinib and Nilotinib: These

By connecting the energy distribution machinery and the data-driven devices, maintenance and record-keeping can be automated and streamlined. texts.full_text: the Bat light, we pray, we look for someone to rescue us. We search for stories of survivors, of helpers. We look for those beacons of hope that we will get through this, that we are being helped. Difficult times bring out thebest in people and Captain Tom is a shining example. In these uncertain days when our world feels fragile — where social distancing has become both necessity as well an emotional distance from one another - it's comforting to know there exists someone like him who embodies hope, tenacity & kindness. His actions have not only raised funds but also lifted spirits and shown the power of individual will. Kate Beddow perfectly encapsulates this sentiment in her reflection on Captain Tom’s impact: "We need a Hero," she writes — implying that heroes are precisely what we require during times such as these, to remind us there is still light at end tunnel. His story resonates with the collective human spirit's yearning for inspiration and reassurance amidst turmoil; it serves not just his nation but all of humanity. While CaptainTom has certainly stepped into this role admirably in recent weeks — a testament that heroes can emerge from anywhere, evenwithin our homes or gardens during lockdowns. His storyis about resilience against age-old challenges: the battle between hope and despair. His achievement stands as proof for us all to believe we are capable of extraordinary deeds when faced with adversity. In conclusion: Captain Tom has become a symbol not just because he raised money, but due his embodiment qualities that define heroism — courage in challenging circumstances; selflessness by giving backduring times others may be more

texts.full_text: years, some IT professional (especially people who developed smart phone system) were proposing using SHA-1 as the hash function for [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Signaling_System_7](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Signaling_System_7) , which is used to implement roaming in mobile system. Of course, if the attack is practical, anyone could create rogue cell tower and listen to phone conversation without any secret keys. If you read the Phrack paper and digest the ideas presented there, you would realize how a practical attack on SHA-1 could be implemented. At the same time, it was not a trivial attack, and you would have to understand advanced mathematics before you can understand the paper. The Phrack paper was meant to be a warning to all IT professional in the world to stop using SHA-1 because it was not as secure as previously thought. Of course, people who do not read the paper and do not understand advanced mathematics would not understand the attack, so they dismissed the paper and considered it as irrelevant and not important. Unfortunately, the attack on SHA-1 did eventually become practical in 2017, so now, everyone in the world can implement the attack on SHA-1 with [https://shattered.it](https://shattered.it) tool. Of course, the people who developed smart phone roaming system also realized that they should not use SHA-1 in their system, so they fixed the problem quickly. Unfortunately, all the other IT professional did not get the warning so they are still using SHA-1 for cryptography signature system. There’s an interesting hypothesis that part of the reason for the drop in the crime rate since the 1970s is that we took lead out of gasoline; inner city youth grew up lead-poisoned, which can make people stupid as well as violent. IQ is still under debate, as it’s a locally nfluence, culturally nfluenced construct. What that translates to, is, very little in terms of real brain science is used to determine IQ. Just tests. There’s much literature on Q tests demonstratng some child’s IQ as seemingly low, when under different testing, it’s shown to be high. Cohen was one of the many attendees of the now-legendary 42nd Street Theater — the planet’s most famous grindhouse theater — which often showed double-bills of the same questionable films he would later grow up to make, and which also inspired Tarantino and Rodriquez.C.H.U.D. (1984) perfectly embodies this gritty spirit, set against the backdrop of New York's grimy streets and subway systems during a time when crime rates were high, and urban decay was rampant. The film follows an ambitious young police officer named Rob Mitchell as he investigates bizarre disappearances linked to homeless individuals who have been transformed into monstrous creatures by a secret government experiment gone awry. Cohen's unique blend of social commentary with his trademark mix of horror, comedy, and action elements is on full display in C.H.U.D. The film not only serves as an entertaining piece of genre cinema but also offers incisive observations about the societal neglect faced by homeless people during this era—a group often rendered invisible or feared by the general public. By transforming these marginalized individuals into literal monsters, Cohen draws attention to their plight while simultaneously crafting a thrilling narrative that keeps audiences on edge throughout its runtime.
